title: hygrophila angustifolia var. phlomoides
intro: Hygrophila angustifolia var. phlomoides, also known as the Indian waterwort or water hyssop, is a perennial herb that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hygrophila angustifolia var. phlomoides include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used as a topical application for its antiseptic properties.
